detaileddescription.para: Sets of recognised values for string-type session parameters.

briefdescription.para: Non-emulator backends.
Backends that support GPU execution.
Backends that support noise.
Backends that only support noise, i.e. will not run with noise = false.
Valid AER simulator types.
Recommended maximum qubit number for selected accelerator type.
Recognised noise mitigation strategies.
Valid placement strategies.
Valid measurement sampling options.
Supported circuit input sources.
The number of shots remaining to be drawn from results_ using draw_shot.
The number of shots to be run on the current process (differs from sn if running with multiple MPI processes).

The results of a Quantum calculation. The map value (count) is the number of times the map key (Qubit states with the same bit indexation as the quantum processor registers) was measured after all shots were run.

simplesect.para:
The Qubit states are represented as a vector of booleans for the reasons outlined below:
High qubit counts can quickly exhaust all possible values able to be encoded by even a 64-bit integer, causing integer overflow.
Values are agnostic with respect to both endianness and ordering because a vector with indices correspond unambiguously to the register number of each qubit. Specifically:
Ordering: if qubits were saved as e.g. a string "0010110", a convention about whether the value of qubit 0 goes on the left of the string or the right of it must be chosen.
Endianness: if instead a maximally compact representation is opted for where an integer that corresponds to the whole bitstring is used (e.g. all_bitstring_counts_), a convention about whether bit 0 is the most or the least significant bit in encoding that integer must be chosen.

briefdescription.para: Provides counts for every possible combination of qubit measurements, ordered according to the selected encoding (MSB, LSB). Requires calc_all_bitstring_counts to be set to true.

detaileddescription.para: E.g. If 2 qubits are used in the calculation, the out counts vector will contain the count of each result in the below order.

simplesect.para: The mapping from bitstring to vector index can be obtained from the function bitstring_index.

briefdescription.para: Probability distribution of output results. Order of probabilities is identical to the order of the all_bitstring_counts_.

simplesect.para: The mapping from bitstring to vector index can be obtained from the function bitstring_index.

briefdescription.para: A 2D array of probability gradients with dimensions of the number of parameters by the number of qubits. Requires calc_gradients to be set to true.

detaileddescription.para: This array stores gradients of the bitstring probabilities with respect to the circuit parameters, in the following format (where y is the probability list and x is the parameter list):

As the gradients are returned as a list-of-lists, they can be accessed in row major format, and indexing the above matrix can be done accordingly, i.e. all_bitstring_probability_gradients()[0][1] corresponds to the dy_0/dx_1 value. x_i correspond to the parameters set using circuit_parameters() (i.e. the parameters ordered by their first appearance in the circuit). y_i are the output probabilities of different bitstrings, indexed in the same manner as all_bitstring_counts_. Explicitly, the index i corresponding to a specific bitstring can be obtained by calling bitstring_index(bitstring), with bitstring given as a list of bit values.

briefdescription.para: The XACC accelerator in use.
State vector from qpp or aer.
Whether or not to apply SPAM error mitigation.
When error mitigation is performed for the session, the raw results are stored in here.
Full path to an input QASM source file.
An input QASM source string.
Provides a xacc::CompositeInstruction IR target.

detaileddescription.para: If the IR target is provided instead of QASM strings or files, the QASM compilation step will be skipped. The IR target can be manually constructed by building the IR tree using XACC.

briefdescription.para: List of GPU device numbers.
A CUDAQ kernel to execute, with any free parameters already fully specified.

detaileddescription.para: Path to an OpenQASM file to include at the beginning of every circuit Contains custom Quantum Brilliance gate definitions.

briefdescription.para: Vector of circuit parameters to use for runtime substitution.
Whether or not gradients will be calculated for parametrized circuits.
Whether or not a non-compact output counts vector will be calculated.
The path to the remote backend database yaml file. The path to a YAML file with configuration data for remote backends (including hardware).
Chosen backend accelerator.
The simulator type for the AER backend accelerator.
The maximum number of OpenMP threads that AER can use.
The depth of random circuit to be generated.
The frontend language in which the input circuit is written.
Enable noisy simulation.
A noise mitigation method to apply.
Correction matrix to use for SPAM error correction.
Disable circuit placement IR transformations (both pure topological and noise-based placement).
The name of the module to use for circuit placement IR transformation.
Disable circuit optimization IR transformations.
Circuit optimization passes to apply.
Whether or not to actually execute the circuit upon calling run.

detaileddescription.para: Set false to disable circuit simulation, for e.g., inspecting transpilation or resource estimation only.

briefdescription.para: Compute the state vector with qpp or aer.
Enable output transpilation and resource estimation.
Disable timing estimation.
The number of qubits to simulate.
The number of measurement shots to be performed.

detaileddescription.para: Seed value for the session's random number generator. Zero indicates that the seed should be generated by std::random_device.

briefdescription.para: Initial bond dimension for tensor network simulators.

simplesect.para: This is only needed when using tensor network backend accelerators.

briefdescription.para: Maximum bond dimension for tensor network simulators.

simplesect.para: This is only needed when using tensor network backend accelerators.

briefdescription.para: Initial Kraus dimension for the purification simulator.

simplesect.para: This is only needed when using the emulator's purification backend accelerator.

briefdescription.para: Maximum Kraus dimension for the purification simulator.

simplesect.para: This is only needed when using the emulator's purification backend accelerator.

briefdescription.para: Absolute SVD cutoff for tensor network simulators.

simplesect.para: This is only needed when using tensor network backend accelerators.

briefdescription.para: Relative SVD cutoff for tensor network simulators.

simplesect.para: This is only needed when using tensor network backend accelerators.

briefdescription.para: Set the measurement sampling method. Options: "cutensornet" uses the single-shot cutensorNet contraction method of the entire tensor network state. Program terminates with error meassage if cutensorNet fails. "cutensornet_multishot" uses the multi-shot cutensorNet contraction method. "sequential" uses the cutensor sequential contraction method. "auto" (default) uses the cutensorNet contraction method and automatically swithes to the cutensor sequential contraction method if the cutensorNet method fails.

simplesect.para: This is only needed if using a tensor network accelerator

briefdescription.para: Noise model to use when noise = true.
Debug mode (verbose logging)
Construct a new session object.

detaileddescription.para: Some parameters are uninitialized, e.g., number of qubits (qn). These parameters can be set manually.

briefdescription.para: Construct a new session object using a specific ordering for all_bitstring_counts_ and associated QML-relevant outputs.

parameterdescription.para: MSB flag. If true, use MSB to determine ordering of all_bitstring_counts_ all_bitstring_probabilities and all_bitstring_probability_gradients table; else use LSB.

briefdescription.para: Create fully specified input CUDAQ kernel from a parameterised kernel and some argument values at which to evaluate it.

parameterdescription.para: Input CUDAQ kernel (templated callable returning void)
Concrete argument values with which to call the CUDAQ kernel

memberdef.definition: const std::map< std::vector< bool >, int > & qristal::session::results
memberdef.name: results

briefdescription.para: Get the output measurement counts as a map.

simplesect.para: Measurement counts map

memberdef.definition: const std::map< std::vector< bool >, int > & qristal::session::results_native
memberdef.name: results_native

briefdescription.para: Get the native output measurement counts as a map.

detaileddescription.para:
Native measurement counts map
Beware: The native results are only stored separately, if a confusion or correction matrix was supplied to session, enabling automatic SPAM correction!

briefdescription.para: Get the full state vector (works with qpp and aer backends only!)

simplesect.para: Full complex state vector as std::vector<std::complex<double>>

briefdescription.para: Get the output measurement counts as a vector.

simplesect.para: Measurement counts vector

briefdescription.para: Get the output probabilities.

simplesect.para: Measurement probabilities vector

briefdescription.para: Get the output probability gradients.

simplesect.para: Table of probability jacobians w.r.t. runtime parameters

briefdescription.para: Get the output transpiled circuit.

simplesect.para: Output transpiled circuit as an OpenQASM string.

briefdescription.para: Get the output Aer QObj JSON string.

simplesect.para: QObj Json string

briefdescription.para: Get the output QB JSON string (QB hardware JSON POST payload)

simplesect.para: QB Json string.

briefdescription.para: Get the number of one-qubit gates applied to each individual qubit.

simplesect.para: Keys: qubit indices; values: number of one-qubit gates.

briefdescription.para: Get the number of two-qubit gates applied to each individual qubit.

simplesect.para: Keys: qubit indices; values: number of two-qubit gates.

briefdescription.para: Get estimated circuit execution times on hardware.

simplesect.para: Estimated hardware runtimes, in ms. Keys: 0: Total time 1: Initialisation component 2: Gate (max. depth) component 3: Readout component 4: Total time (from classical simulation) 5: PC transfer to controller time

briefdescription.para: Get the output expected value in the Z basis, from the shot counts observed.

simplesect.para: Expected value in the Z basis

briefdescription.para: Set the SPAM correction matrix by providing an equivalent SPAM confusion matrix.
Retrieve an equivalent confusion matrix from the SPAM correction matrix.
Validate the run i.e. ensure all configurations are set in a valid manner.
Execute a standard SPAM benchmark, and use the measured confusion matrix to automatically correct SPAM errors in a consecutive run()

detaileddescription.para:
Arguments:
n_shots
: The number of shots to be used for the SPAM benchmark. Defaults to 0, taking the same number of shots as set in sns_.
Execute all quantum tasks Returns a job handle if the job is posted to a remote accelerator (e.g. AWS Braket). Otherwise, returns null if this function completes the run locally.

briefdescription.para: Cancel any in-flight asynchronous execution of run()
Returns the (base-10) integer vector index for the probabilities/ counts vector, corresponding to a bitstring.

parameterdescription.para: The bit-vector to be converted to the vector index

briefdescription.para: Randomly draw (and remove) a single shot from the results map.
Try to work out the form of the circuit input.

detaileddescription.para: Forms checked first get precedence; fields associated with other forms get ignored as soon as a valid form is found.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::shared_ptr< xacc::CompositeInstruction > kernel_ir, std::shared_ptr< xacc::Accelerator > sim_qpu, const xacc::HeterogeneousMap &sim_qpu_configs, std::shared_ptr< xacc::AcceleratorBuffer > buffer_b, double runtime_ms, std::shared_ptr< qristal::backend > qb_transpiler)
memberdef.name: process_run_result

briefdescription.para: Helper to populate result tables (e.g. counts, expectation values, resource estimations) post-execution.
Util method to compile input source string into IR.

detaileddescription.para: This method is thread-safe, thus can be used to compile multiple source strings in parallel.

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the target circuit string. This will involve loading a file, generating a random circuit string, etc, depending on the value of input_origin.

parameterdescription.para: The origin of the input circuit.

simplesect.para: The target circuit as a std::string.

briefdescription.para: Combine all backend options into a dict (xacc::HeterogeneousMap).

simplesect.para: A xacc::HeterogeneousMap containing the settings for the backend in use.

briefdescription.para: Get the simulator.

Calculate the gradients for the parametrized quantum task. This will calculate the gradients of the probabilities of all possible output bitstrings of the circuit, with respect to each circuit parameter. The session does this by creating two new session objects and using them to compute the gradients using the "parameter-shift" rule, where the circuit is run again using slightly shifted parameters.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::shared_ptr< xacc::Accelerator > acc, std::shared_ptr< xacc::AcceleratorBuffer > buffer_b, std::shared_ptr< xacc::CompositeInstruction > &circuit)
memberdef.name: execute_on_simulator

briefdescription.para: Execute the circuit on a simulator.

detaileddescription.para: Populate a given counts map with results from QPU execution. Templated measure_counts_map to support different type of map-like data.

Wrap raw OpenQASM string in a QB Kernel:
Move qreg to a kernel argument
Denote the kernel name as 'qristal_circuit'

briefdescription.para: A session of the Qristal SDK.
